ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT MR. MORGAN WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PACKING FROM ORIGINAL CRANE CO. VALVES OR THAT SUCH EXPOSURE WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR CONTRIBUTING TO HIS RISK OF DEVELOPING AN ASBESTOS-RELATED DISEASE. MR. MORGAN'S DEPOSITION AND WRITTEN DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF MR. MCLEAN AND MR. PANTALEONI IDENTIFIED AND DISCUSSED IN PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY RESPONSES PRECLUDE DEFENDANT FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. EVEN IF DEFENDANT SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION, MR. MORGAN'S DECLARATION AND DEFENDANT'S RESPONSES TO GENERAL ORDER 129 INTERROGATORIES CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. MORGAN WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING CRANITE. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO MR. MORGAN'S DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Robert Morgan

RULING:
DEFENDANT SPIRAX SARCO, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Spirax Sarco, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Morgan was exposed to asbestos-containing gaskets original to Sarco steam traps. Mr. Morgan's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant did sustain its burden, Mr. Morgan's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing gaskets original to Sarco steam traps. The only issue raised by the summary adjudication motion is punitive damages, which plaintiff says he has dismissed. Order signed in open court. RULING:
DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Morgan was exposed to asbestos-containing packing from original Crane Co. valves or that such exposure was a substantial factor contributing to his risk of developing an asbestos-related disease. Mr. Morgan's deposition and written discovery responses and the deposition testimony of Mr. McLean and Mr. Pantaleoni identified and discussed in plaintiff's discovery responses preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant satisfied its burden of production, Mr. Morgan's declaration and defendant's responses to General Order 129 interrogatories create a triable issue whether Mr. Morgan was exposed to asbestos-containing Cranite. Defendant's objections to Mr. Morgan's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court.Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
